checks.finding: REQ-006 through REQ-017 define observable artifacts, content inventories, interfaces, diagnostics, exit states, compatibility behavior, hash-binding behavior, and approval-state transitions; AC-001 through AC-016 plus the behavioral and layer contracts provide measurable validation outcomes.

Every goal REQ-006 through REQ-017 is mapped to at least one acceptance criterion in acceptance-tests.md; the table explicitly covers all goal IDs, including cross-cutting parity, LITE, review-boundary, release, and approval requirements.

AC-001 through AC-016 specify inspectable files, required content, stable diagnostics, exit codes, manifest hashes, rejection behavior, version synchronization, and task-state transitions; the behavioral contracts further define concrete inputs and expected results.

The Non-goals section explicitly excludes Figma integration, automated visualization, new review stages, sdd-ship and quality-gate changes, Mermaid rendering, and a backend layer file; REQ-013 and the edge cases separately preserve the LITE and legacy repository-only boundaries.

Material constraints are explicit: REQ-012 defines canonical feature syntax and traversal-safe failure behavior, REQ-014 requires Bash/PowerShell parity, REQ-016 requires canonical hash-bound review inputs, REQ-017 preserves human or signed-sudo approval, and the Security Boundaries table addresses local visual data and path trust boundaries.

The stated risks have planned validation surfaces: AC-015 covers canonical-path and tamper rejection, AC-011 and TEST-019 preserve explicit repository-only compatibility, AC-009 and the LITE contract cover interview/LITE behavior, and AC-014 checks synchronized release metadata and changelog state.
